content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance        Administration          Executive Legal          Ethics Training          Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660             322-5660               322-5501                 322-6441               322-6444

August 17, 1981

Nancy C. Miller, Esq.
Law Offices of Richard H. Hyde, Inc.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                                Re:  Capitol Area Development
                                                     Authority

Dear Nancy:

     This letter is sent to confirm your letter of July 17,
1981, and our phone conversations. As previously determined,
CADA is a "state agency" within the provisions of Government
Code Sections 82004 and 86100-86300 pursuant to Commission
Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18249. Accordingly,
lobbying activities on CADA's behalf are exempt from the
lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5401.

                                                Very truly yours,


                                                Diane Maura Fishburg
                                                Counsel
                                                Legal Division

DMF:plh

LAW OFFICES
OF
RICHARD H. HYDE, INC.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, California 95814
Telephone: (916) 447-7933

July 17, 1981

Diane Fishburn, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Capitol Area Development Authority

Dear Diane:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation of July 14,
1981, enclosed please find Capitol Area Development
Authority documents as follows:

1. Joint Powers Agreement between the City of
Sacramento and State of California dated
July, 1978.

2. Letter to Dwight Dickerson dated April 23,
1979, confirming a telephone conversation
regarding CADA's status as a "State Agency".

As we discussed, our firm is retained as the Agency's
counsel, and we do on occasion perform "lobbyist" activities
on behalf of the Authority. I would appreciate a confirming
letter from you stating that CADA, for the purposes of the
FPPC, is a State Agency and that our performance of lobbying
functions on its behalf are exempt from the Lobbyist
Registration and Reporting Requirements under Government
Code Section 86100 - 86300.

If you need any additional documentation or information,
please contact me.

Very truly yours,

NANCY J. MILLER

NCM:cr

Enclosures

sections.question: Whether the Capitol Area Development Authority (CADA) is a 'state agency' within the provisions of Government Code Sections 82004 and 86100-86300, and whether lobbying activities on CADA's behalf are exempt from lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.
sections.conclusion: CADA is a 'state agency' within the provisions of Government Code Sections 82004 and 86100-86300 pursuant to Commission Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18249. Accordingly, lobbying activities on CADA's behalf are exempt from the lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.
sections.question_synthetic: Is the Capitol Area Development Authority a state agency exempt from lobbyist registration and reporting requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that CADA qualifies as a state agency under applicable law and therefore lobbying activities performed on its behalf are exempt from lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (July 17, 1981 letter from Nancy Miller) and the FPPC response (August 17, 1981 letter from Diane Maura Fishburg). No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but the structure clearly indicates the legal question and FPPC's answer. Confidence is high based on clear statement of legal determination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Capitol Area Development Authority (CADA) is a 'state agency' within the provisions of Government Code Sections 82004 and 86100-86300, and whether lobbying activities on CADA's behalf are exempt from lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.

CONCLUSION: CADA is a 'state agency' within the provisions of Government Code Sections 82004 and 86100-86300 pursuant to Commission Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18249. Accordingly, lobbying activities on CADA's behalf are exempt from the lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to an inquiry from counsel for the Capitol Area Development Authority, confirming that CADA is a state agency and that lobbying activities performed on its behalf are exempt from lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under Government Code Sections 86100-86300.
